mnemonics: RDBMS: Really Doesn't Belong in Massive-Scale. Think SCV - Scale, Complexity, Variety. RDBMS can't handle these three V's of Big Data.
keyPoints: RDBMS is designed for structured data and transactional integrity, not for the massive volume of unstructured data in Big Data.
The rigid, schema-on-write structure of an RDBMS is too inflexible for the variety and velocity of data sources in Big Data analytics.
RDBMS scaling is primarily vertical (scale-up), which is cost-prohibitive and has physical limits, whereas Big Data systems scale horizontally (scale-out) across clusters of commodity hardware.
commonMistakes: Assuming RDBMS is obsolete. It is still the superior tool for structured data and ACID-compliant transactions, just not for Big Data analytics.
Believing the problem is solely about storage size. The core issues are the system's architecture, flexibility, and scalability model.
